reviews.review_msg: U can sleep in his lecture and by doing homework, u can get an A.

The result of SFU's budget cuts. Talks to his page which he projects onto the screen all class long and occasionally looks up every 20 minutes to check if his muddled speech has put students to sleep. Skip Joe's classes and just watch MIT's amazing Arthur Mattuck in 18.03 (Spring 2006). Explains clearly, in depth, and in fewer/shorter lectures.

Joe knows the material well but sometimes explains the material in a manner that is slightly harder to understand for someone just beginning to learn the material. If you have any questions, he's willing to help and he does try his best to explain things.

Joe is an amazing prof! He's very clear on his explanations and gave a fair midterm and final! He tells you what you're going to be tested on exactly and the types of questions! Would definitely take again and I recommend anyone to take this class with him!
Please for god sake don't take his class.
lectures are not useful, and hard to understand his accent,  never ever take his course.

Confusing lectures. He copies off the textbook and just reads off it during class. Unfortunately, the textbook often likes to assume things are obvious (usually its not), and he does not provide any further explanation during class.  Also, He talks very very fast, which makes his heavy accent even more difficult to understand.
difficulty of exams are fair, BUT this prof is useless. Come to lectures or not doesn't make any difference.

Absolutely USELESS lectures. This is how I learn for this class: I watch youtube vids to learn the material, come to class and sit near the back doing the homework during Monday and Wednesday lectures, come on Fridays 5min before the quiz to write it and leave. Thank you Khan Academy, Patrick JMT and IntegralCALC for the USEFUL lectures.

Joe is completely useless as a professor. He rarely ever explains the intentions behind what he is doing and constantly makes mistakes while doing it. If you see him as your professor, just avoid the class and take it with someone else. Jamie Mulholland is 100 times better.
never. just never. The thing that he is doing in class can never define as teach and the system does not let me rate 0

Your best bet is to form a study group and teach each other, because this prof is exceptionally useless in lectures.   Teaming up with a group and a Chegg account is probably the best way to get through this!
He has a bad accent and unorganized notes. Cannot see what he is doing in class. Worst prof I have ever had.

This may sound rude but he is the absolute worst prof I have had to date. Not only is it next to impossible to understand him, but the notes are so confusing and disorganized that you might as read the back of a cereal box for help. If you have this class with him, drop it so you don't waste your money.

I have never encountered lectures that were as useless as the ones Joe gives. He essentially reads scribbled notes on a lined piece of paper which are not legible to anybody but Joe himself. Lectures are pointless, your best bet is to learn by yourself. Run far away from this class, not sure why this is even allowed to exist as a class.
horrible accent and can't teach. If you take this course, you'd better RUN!
This man cannot teach!!! do not even bother showing up for class. The text book is your best friend if you are forced to take his class. my advice. RUN FAST RUN FAR!!

the best prof i've ever taken. exams were easy, hardly even had to go to class. accent was a little hard to understand here and there but for the most part he was very willing to answer questions. i would take him again.

well...math157 IS HARD. I got a c+ even tho i put lot of effort. BUT HOWEVER, we should see that coming in math157. He is a nice person, tho, even tho his writing is kinda bad, but once you remember the way he right, it will be fine. Suggestion to math157: work hard, hard, harder!! lol

Never reviewed a prof but I have to cus he is that bad. Lecs are horrible and useless cus he just writes w/out explaining the steps + he has super tiny writing which doesn't help at all. Doing txtbk questions won't help cus his exams are WAY more difficult than what you see on the assignments + textbook. Wording on exams are ambiguous. Avoid him.

WORST prof I've ever had, I know this sounds harsh but unfortunately I stopped going to lectures because he was so bad at teaching, his accent definitely made things worse , and he literary copies the notes on the board and thats it. I cant believe I actually paid for this class not to mention his exams are so unreasonably hard.
Writing is very small, midterm was noting like the previous midterms, much more difficult. Makes mistakes on his lecture slides.

Horrible Prof,                new to SFU,                       hard exams and doesn't curve,      Just simply copies his notes onto overhead for and hour and  leaves including his mistakes                                Recommend not slacking and studying from textbook and you'll walk away with a decent grade, i doubt there will be any A+'s

Worst prof I've had. Teaches terribly, doesn't cover all the material, hard exams. Worst grade I've ever got, and the course itself is not that difficult. Should have skipped his lectures and studied on my own instead
I cant believe I am paying this amount of tuition for this kind of professor.
Worst math prof ever. His writing is illegible. He mumbles and is very hard to understand even when speaking clearly. He basically just fills in his slide notes and doesn't try to actually explain.

I haven't been with a prof., that teaches calculus by filling in the blank, he is a horrible prof. he goes over the material very fast, that is even if he goes through all the material!! i wish i took the course with someone else.
